title: Chapter 8 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Vectors, Strings, and Hash Maps!

questions.question: What is the primary difference between Arrays and Vectors?
options: Arrays are fixed-size (Stack), Vectors can dynamically grow (Heap)
questions.explanation: Vectors exist fully on the heap to dynamically expand.

questions.question: The macro used to instantly create a new Vector with starting values is `________![]`.
Why is calling `.get(index)` on a Vector safer than using raw bracket `[index]` notation?
options: It returns an `Option` (`Some` or `None`) instead of panicking on out-of-bounds access.
It automatically adds the item if it's missing
questions.explanation: `.get()` protects you from out-of-bounds crashes by returning `None` instead of panicking.

questions.question: Under the hood, a Rust `String` is actually just a wrapper dynamically holding a `Vec<u8>` (Vector of bytes).
questions.explanation: Strings are secretly Vectors disguised effectively as text.

questions.question: Why does Rust forbid you from doing `let letter = my_string[0];`?
options: Because UTF-8 characters can vary radically in byte length (1 to 4 bytes), an index of '0' might return invalid half-characters.
Strings only live on the Stack
questions.explanation: UTF-8 complexity breaks simple indexing assumptions.

questions.question: To aggressively add string data directly onto the end of an existing dynamically mutable `String`, you successfully utilize `.________("data")`.
questions.explanation: `.push_str()` natively adds string slices onto dynamically Heap strings.

questions.question: If you dynamically need to map string "Blue" cleanly to integer 10, essentially building a fast dictionary, what Collection do you uniquely use?
questions.explanation: HashMaps rigorously map explicit keys to absolute values perfectly natively.

questions.question: Just definitively like the String structure, explicitly utilizing a precisely populated `HashMap` fundamentally requires exclusively importing dynamically it into scope cleanly utilizing exactly `use std::collections::HashMap;`.
questions.explanation: Unlike Strings or Vectors uniquely embedded seamlessly dynamically natively in standard prelude smoothly, HashMaps essentially exclusively require manual rigorous inclusion.
